Name: Muro'jin & Nekraxx
Summary: Dual-boss opener that demands even health, trap routing, and disease-aware defensive play.
Description:
[Phase 1] Split damage evenly so Muro'jin never gets to Revive Pet and Nekraxx never snowballs with Bestial Wrath.
[Phase 2] Use Freezing Traps only for Carrion Swoop, because stunning Nekraxx with an ice block is the safest way to absorb the charge without clipping the group.
[Phase 3] Use personals for Infected Pinions or Barrage overlaps and keep clear of Fetid Quillstorm until both bosses die together.

Name: Vordaza
Summary: Attrition boss with phantom collisions, tank channels, frontal dodges, and shield breaks.
Description:
[Phase 1] Dodge Unmake and keep Withering Miasma healing planned from pull onward.
[Phase 2] Kite Wrest Phantoms into each other in pairs, never detonate too many at once, and avoid standing in the pools they leave behind.
[Phase 3] Tanks use defensives for Drain Soul and the group saves damage to break Necrotic Convergence before the channel resolves.

Name: Rak'tul, Vessel of Souls
Summary: Final soul boss with constant aura damage, grouped totem spawns, and tank knockback combos.
Description:
[Phase 1] Heal through Deathgorged Vessel while sidestepping Volatile Essence circles and preserving space.
[Phase 2] During Crush Souls, the three targets group tightly so Soulbind Totems spawn together, then the party swaps to destroy the totems before the pull effect spirals.
[Phase 3] Tanks move Spiritbreaker away from the group, drop the puddle cleanly, and use mobility or front braziers to survive the knockback.
